back_translation: The beginning of the good news concerning Jesus Christ.
As was written in the book of Isaiah the prophet: 'Behold, we will send our messenger ahead of you, he will prepare your way.'
'The voice of one crying in the wilderness: prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.'
John the Baptizer appeared in the wilderness, proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
People from all the land of Judea and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem went out to him, and while confessing their sins, they received baptism from him in the Jordan River.
John was clothed with cloth made from camel's hair, and had a leather belt tied around his waist; his food was locusts and wild honey.
He proclaimed: 'One more powerful than I is coming after me, whose sandal strap I am not even worthy to bend down and untie.'
I give baptism to you all with water, but He will give baptism to you all with the Holy Spirit.
At that time, Jesus came from the town of Nazareth in the region of Galilee, and was baptized by John in the Jordan River.
As soon as He came up from the water, He beheld the sky being torn open, and the Spirit descended to rest upon Him like a dove.
And there was a divine voice from heaven: 'You are my beloved son, I am exceedingly pleased with you.'
And immediately the Spirit drove Jesus into the wilderness.
He was in the wilderness forty days, being tempted by Satan, was with the wild animals, and the angels came to serve Him.
After John was arrested, Jesus went into the region of Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God.
He said: 'The appointed time has come, and the kingdom of God is near; repent and believe in the good news.'
While Jesus was walking along the Sea of Galilee, He beheld Simon and Andrew his brother casting nets in the sea, for they were fishermen.
Jesus said to them: 'Come follow me, and I will make you catchers of people.'
Immediately, they abandoned the nets and followed Him.
Continuing on a little farther, He beheld James son of Zebedee and John his brother, in a boat mending nets.
Immediately He called them, and they abandoned Zebedee their father in the boat with the hired workers, and followed Him.
They entered the city of Capernaum, and on the Sabbath day He entered the synagogue and began to teach.
They were astonished at His teaching, because He taught them as one who has authority, not like the scribes.
In the synagogue there was a man with an unclean spirit indwelling him; he cried out immediately,
'What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — you are the Holy One of God!'
Jesus rebuked it: 'Be silent, and come out of him!'
The unclean spirit caused him to convulse, and cried out with a loud voice, then came out of him.
Everyone was stunned, and they asked one another: 'What is this? A new teaching with authority! Even the unclean spirits, He commands, and they obey Him.'
His fame spread immediately throughout every place in the whole region of Galilee.
As soon as they left the synagogue, they entered the house of Simon and Andrew, together with James and John.
Simon's mother-in-law was lying sick with fever, and immediately they reported about her to Him.
He went near, took hold of her hand, lifted her up, and the fever left her, and she served them.
When evening came, after the sun had set, people brought all the sick and those possessed by demons to Him.
And the whole town gathered at the door of the house.
He healed many who were sick with various diseases, and drove out many demons; but He did not allow the demons to speak, because they knew Him.
Early in the morning while it was still very dark, He rose, went out to a solitary place, and prayed there.
Simon and those with him went out searching for Him.
When they found Him, they reported to Him: 'Everyone is searching for you.'
He said to them: 'Let us go elsewhere, to the neighboring small towns, so that we may proclaim there also; for this reason we came out.'
He went throughout the region of Galilee, proclaiming in their synagogues and driving out demons.
A leper came to Him, kneeling and pleading with Him, saying: 'If you are willing, you can make me clean.'
He was angry, stretched out His hand and touched the man, and said to him: 'I am willing, be clean.'
Immediately the leprosy left him, and he was clean.
He sternly charged him, and sent him out immediately,
saying to him: 'Take care, do not tell anyone anything at all, but go show yourself to the priest, and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing as Moses commanded, as a witness to them.'

But the man went out, began to proclaim and spread this matter widely, so that Jesus could no longer openly enter a town, but stayed out in solitary places; yet people came to Him from every direction.
